title: Floorers and wall tilers
description: Lay and bind building materials, such as brick, structural tile, concrete block, cinder block, glass block, and terra-cotta block, with mortar and other substances, to construct or repair walls, partitions, arches, sewers, and other structures. Lay and install carpet from rolls or blocks on floors. Install padding and trim flooring materials. Apply blocks, strips, or sheets of shock-absorbing, sound-deadening, or decorative coverings to floors. (Based on 9 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Join edges of carpet and seam edges where necessary by sewing or using tape with glue and heated carpet iron.
Cut covering and foundation materials according to blueprints and sketches, and measure and cut metal lath to size for walls and ceilings.
Select and order tile and other items to be installed according to specifications, and assist customers in selection of tile and grout.
Mix and apply mortar or cement to edges and ends of drain tiles, and apply a sealer to make grout stain- and water-resistant.
Brush glue onto manila paper on which design has been drawn, position tiles finished side down onto paper, and cut and shape tile to fit around obstacles.
Sweeping, scraping, sanding, or chipping dirt and irregularities to clean base surfaces, and inspecting surfaces to ensure they are firm and dry.
Roll and press sheet wall and floor covering into cement base to smooth and finish surface, and trim excess covering materials and join sections to form tight joints.
Lay out, position and apply shock-absorbing, sound-deadening, or decorative coverings to floors and walls, determining traffic areas and seams.
Remove excess cement and glue from finished surfaces, and disconnect and remove appliances and worn coverings from floors and walls.
Inspect floors for smoothness, scrape and sand edges inaccessible to floor sanders, and guide sanding machines over surfaces until smooth.
Apply filler compound and coats of finish to floors to seal wood, and mix, apply, and spread mortar, cement, and adhesives to form a bed for the tiles.
Apply mortar to tile back, position the tile, and press or tap to affix tile to base, then finish and dress the joints and wipe excess grout from between tiles.
Remove excess mortar and finish joints for a sealed appearance, while interpreting blueprints to determine specifications and calculate materials required.
Remove any old tile, grout, and adhesive using chisels and scrapers, and clean the surface carefully prior to new installations.
Measure and mark surfaces to be tiled according to blueprints, and install fixtures using hand tools.
Cut tile backing and surface it to required size, and apply and smooth mortar or other mixture over work surface before applying coverings.
Wet surface to prepare for bonding, fill holes and cracks with grout, and ensure a smooth surface using appropriate tools.
Prepare cost and labor estimates based on calculations of time and materials needed for projects, and discuss the design with the client.
